subject: URGENT: Anthropic restricting CDP/OpenClaw — Booker pipeline at structural risk
body:
Quinn,

News update Toby asked me to research. Key facts:

1. April 4 2026: Anthropic blocked OpenClaw from Claude Pro/Max subscriptions. Heavy users must use API keys ($3/$15 per M tokens for Sonnet, $15/$75 for Opus) or extra usage bundles.

2. Reason: 'subscriptions weren't built for the usage patterns of these third-party tools.'

3. EXPANDING: 'Anthropic's enforcement began with OpenClaw on April 4 and will expand to all third-party harnesses in the coming weeks.'

4. OAuth tokens in third-party tools (incl Agent SDK) violate Consumer ToS.

IMPACT ON BOOKER PIPELINE:

We technically don't use OpenClaw — we use native Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 and our scripts talk CDP directly via WebSocket. Yesterday's overnight 3.5h batch-pipeline ran fine. BUT enforcement is expanding to 'all third-party harnesses' so direct CDP from a Claude session may break next.

BOOKER'S CDP-DEPENDENT WORK (at risk):
- Stage 0.5/1/2/4 batch-pipeline
- enrich-organizers.ts
- scan-group-posts.ts
- backfill-hosts.ts

BOOKER'S CDP-FREE WORK (still works inside Claude):
- Stage 3 SQLite processing
- extract-hosts-from-artifacts.ts
- queries, planning, code edits, hub coordination

OTHER PERSONAS:
- Sarah, Cord, Dash, Fulton, Porter, Harvey: UNAFFECTED (no CDP)
- Booker: HEAVILY affected — CDP is core to discovery pipeline
- AIDI: indirectly — discovery metrics will stall if CDP is restricted

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION:

1. SHORT-TERM (this week): User runs CDP scripts in native terminal, NOT via Claude tool calls. Booker stays in Claude for processing/coordination.

2. MEDIUM-TERM (2-4 weeks): ACCELERATE the Dedicated Scraper proposal (DEDICATED-SCRAPER-PROPOSAL-v3 — multi-niche, Mac Mini, AI discovery, trigger API). This was already on roadmap; now blocking infrastructure.

3. LONG-TERM: Booker becomes planner/coordinator persona only. All CDP moves to Mac Mini. Booker (Claude session) requests scrapes via trigger API.

Needs your decision: do we accelerate the dedicated scraper to top priority? It changes how Booker works fundamentally and crosses domains (Booker designs it, but it touches porter/harvey orchestration).

AIDI was sent the same notice via Collab inbox.

— Booker
